faqs.question: Why is tick control important in Morningside Heights?
faqs.answer: Morningside Heights has abundant green spaces including Morningside Park and Riverside Park where ticks thrive. With high foot traffic from Columbia University students, families, and dog walkers, professional tick control protects the community from Lyme disease and other tick-borne illnesses common in these park areas.

faqs.question: How much does tick control cost in Morningside Heights?
faqs.answer: Our tick control services in Morningside Heights range from $125 to $325 depending on property size, infestation severity, and treatment frequency. faqs.question: What areas of Morningside Heights have the most tick problems?
faqs.answer: Properties adjacent to Morningside Park, Riverside Park, and wooded areas along Riverside Drive experience the highest tick activity. Columbia University campus green spaces, community gardens, and tree-lined residential streets between Broadway and Riverside Drive also see significant tick populations during warm months.

faqs.question: When should I schedule tick control in Morningside Heights?
faqs.answer: Tick season in Morningside Heights runs from April through November, with peak activity in late spring and early fall. We recommend scheduling preventive treatments in early spring before ticks become active, with follow-up applications throughout the season to maintain protection for your family and pets.
